prompt: A breathtaking and extremely complex conceptual world-building infographic masterpiece of the "fundamental differences between Confucianism, Taoism, and Buddhism", overall designed as an ancient Eastern classical mythological manuscript. Background: pure white retro textured canvas, light beige aged parchment base color, with slight frayed edges and water stain effects. Core layout: The central visual adopts a grand "vertical egg-shaped layered structure", with Buddhism, Taoism, and Confucianism layers from top to bottom. Edge details: four corners adorned with fine microscopic illustrations themed on ancient observation notes, ritual implements, and runes. Color: low-saturation sage green, light gold, and off-white as main tones, generally light and soft without piercing high-saturation colors. Details: architectural lines, landscape brushwork techniques, lotus patterns, and cloud layers are clearly visible and rendered with extreme detail. Seamless fusion: Natural transitions between the three layers via clouds and flowing water, seamlessly connecting Buddhist light, Taoist Taiji clouds, and Confucian scholarly atmosphere. Style: classical ink line art + low-saturation digital watercolor, with a light Chinese style ancient book manuscript texture. Text annotations: Pure traditional Chinese characters in a mottled ancient Song font, each annotation containing a short title + a line of poetic description, connected to details by dark gold hairline leads with no repetitive pointing. Aspect ratio: 3:4 vertical independent complete unit. Title Area (top): `Confucianism, Buddhism, Taoism: Fundamental Differences`. Central layered annotations: Top layer 'Buddhism': `Buddhism`, `Relationship between self and self`, `No-self, governing the heart, letting go`. Middle layer 'Taoism': `Taoism`, `Relationship between self and all things`, `Non-action, governing the body, keeping an open mind`. Bottom layer 'Confucianism': `Confucianism`, `Relationship between people`, `No-ego, governing the world, taking responsibility`. Side auxiliary annotations: Left: `Purity: clear heart and bright mind, severing afflictions`; `Stillness: following nature, returning to the original heart`; `Reverence: awe of responsibility, active engagement with the world`. Right: `60+ years refining the heart: looking past gains and losses, returning to the inner self`; `35-55 years being human: balance of tension and relaxation, following laws`; `7-35 years doing things: forging ahead with determination, building a career`. Bottom summary: `The balance between being in the world and being out of it is a high-level wisdom of life`.
